zh.lifeSummary: 當Berthold IV. Herzog von Zähringen und Burgund於大約1125年出生在德國爾特波格奧拉赤 武拉賀武拉賀時，他的父親Conrad I von Zähringen34歲，母親Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur16歲。他於1155年與Heilwig von Frohburg在德國結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他於1152年被列為regentschaft als herzog。他去世於1186年12月8日在瑞士福包格福利布格享年62歲，埋葬在德國巴登-爾特波格布萊斯高。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Berthold IV. Herzog von Zähringen und Burgund于大约1125年出生在德国爾特波格奧拉赤 武拉賀武拉賀时，他的父亲Conrad I von Zähringen34岁，母亲Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur16岁。他于1155年与Heilwig von Frohburg在德国结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他于1152年被列为regentschaft als herzog。他去世于1186年12月8日在瑞士福包格福利布格享年62岁，埋葬在德国巴登-爾特波格布萊斯高。

nameMeanings.name: Herzog
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Erwin, Fritz, Heinz, Kurt, Otto, Wolfgang, Franz, Arno, Ernst, Hildegarde, Reinhold.

German: from the Middle High German title of nobility herzoge ‘duke’ (Old High German herizoho, from heri ‘army’ + ziohan ‘to lead’, a calque of the Byzantine title stratēlatēs ‘general, commander’, from Greek stratos ‘army’ + elaunein ‘to lead’). The name is unlikely to refer to descent from an actual duke; it is normally an occupational name for the servant of a duke or a nickname for one who put on the airs and graces of a duke. This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine), Poland, Czechia, and Slovenia (see also 3 below). Compare Hertzog .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German Herzog ‘duke’ (see 1 above). Compare Graf and Kaiser .

variants.name: Hartsook
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herceg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Croatian Stanko.

Croatian: nickname from herceg ‘duke’, a title of the medieval feudal lords of Herzegovina (‘duke's land’, a region in Bosnia and Herzegovina), derived from the Hungarian form of German Herzog . Compare Erceg .

variants.name: Herczeg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Imre, Laszlo, Tibor, Attila, Ferenc, Gaza, Istvan, Janos.
Hungarian form of German   Herzog  .

variants.name: Herzig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erna, Fritzi, Hans, Horst, Markus, Monika.
German (eastern and central Germany and Silesia, Switzerland): variant of   Herzog  .
German: from a short form of any of several ancient Germanic personal names composed with hard ‘strong, hardy’, for instance Herto.

variants.name: Hertzog
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of Herzog and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this. The surname Hertzog is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine).

variants.name: Hartzog
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog   1.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): variant of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsock
meanings: Probably an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsoe
meanings: Perhaps an altered form of   Herzog  .

variants.name: Hartsough
meanings: Americanized form of German   Herzog  .

nameMeanings.name: von
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of Welsh   Vaughan  .
Chinese: variant of   Wong  .
Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: und
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Myer.

Jewish (Ashkenazic): from German Pfund ‘pound’, either a nickname or one of the names chosen at random from vocabulary words in the 18th and 19th centuries by government officials at the time when surnames became compulsory.
Altered form of German   Pfund  .

variants.name: Pfund
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Claus, Erwin, Gerhard.

German: metonymic occupational name for a sealer of weights, or for a wholesale merchant, from Middle High German pfunt ‘pound’ (as a measure of weight and a unit of currency), or a nickname relating to a tax obligation.

nameMeanings.name: Berthold
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: berht ‘bright’ + hold ‘splendid’; alteration of earlier Bertwald, from berht + wald ‘rule, power’.

variants.name: Barthold
meanings: form of   Berthold .

nameMeanings.name: IV.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: When Berthold IV. Herzog von Zähringen und Burgund was born about 1125, in Urach, Urach, Württemberg, Germany, his father, Conrad I von Zähringen, was 34 and his mother, Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur, was 16. He married Heilwig von Frohburg in 1155, in Germany. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 2 daughters. In 1152, his occupation is listed as regentschaft als herzog. He died on 8 December 1186, in Fribourg, Fribourg, Switzerland, at the age of 62, and was buried in Breisgau, Baden-Württemberg, Germany.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Berthold IV. Herzog von Zähringen und Burgund est né environ 1125, à Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Allemagne, son père, Conrad I von Zähringen, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur, avait 16 ans. Il a épousé Heilwig von Frohburg en 1155, à Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 2 filles. En 1152, sa profession de son indiquée est regentschaft als herzog. Il est décédé le 8 décembre 1186, à Fribourg, Fribourg, Suisse, à l’âge de 62 ans, et a été enterré à Breisgau, Baden-Württemberg, Allemagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Berthold IV. Herzog von Zähringen und Burgund um 1125 in Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Conrad I von Zähringen 34 und seine Mutter Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur 16. Er hat 1155 in Deutschland Heilwig von Frohburg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 2 Töchter. 1152 ist sein Beruf als regentschaft als herzog aufgeführt. Er starb am 8. Dezember 1186 im Alter von 62 Jahren in Fribourg, Freiburg, Schweiz und wurde in Breisgau, Baden-Württemberg, Deutschland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Berthold IV. Herzog von Zähringen und Burgund è nato il circa 1125 nel Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Germania, il suo padre, Conrad I von Zähringen, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur, aveva 16 anni. Lui sposato Heilwig von Frohburg nel 1155, nel Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 2 figlie. Nel 1152, la sua occupazione è indicata come regentschaft als herzog. Morì il 8 dicembre 1186, nel Fribourg, Friburgo, Svizzera, all'età di 62 anni, e fu sepolto a Breisgau, Baden-Württemberg, Germania.

ja.lifeSummary: Berthold IV. Herzog von Zähringen und Burgundが約1125年にドイツウuルッテンバーグオバーアムト ウラチウラチで生まれた時，父Conrad I von Zähringenは34歳，母Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namurは16歳であった。1155年にドイツでHeilwig von Frohburgと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。1152年，職業はregentschaft als herzogと記録されている。彼は1186年12月8日にスイスフレイブルグフリボウルグで62歳で死去し，ドイツバデン-ウuルッテンバーグブレイスガウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Berthold IV. Herzog von Zähringen und Burgund(이)가 대략 1125년 독일 wu르테버그 오베램트 라치 라치에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Conrad I von Zähringen(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur(은)는 16세였다. 그는 1155년 독일에서 Heilwig von Frohburg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 1152년, 그의 직업은 regentschaft als herzog이었다. 그는 1186년 12월 8일 스위스 프레이버그 프리보우르그에서 62세의 나이로 사망했으며 독일 바데-wu르테버그 브레이스가에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Berthold IV. Herzog von Zähringen und Burgund nasceu aproximadamente 1125, em Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Alemanha, seu pai, Conrad I von Zähringen, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur, tinha 16 anos. Ele casou-se com Heilwig von Frohburg em 1155, em Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 2 filhas. Em 1152, sua profissão aparece como regentschaft als herzog. Ele faleceu em 8 de dezembro de 1186, em Fribourg, Freiburg, Suiça, com 62 anos, e foi sepultado em Breisgau, Baden-Württemberg, Alemanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Berthold IV. Herzog von Zähringen und Burgund родился около 1125 г. в местности урэич, обирамт урэич, вuрттембирг, Германия, его отцу по имени Conrad I von Zähringen было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur, 16. Он женился на женщине по имени Heilwig von Frohburg в 1155 г. в местности Германия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 2 дочери. В 1152 году он работал по профессии regentschaft als herzog. Он умер 8 декабря 1186 г. в местности фрибург, фрибург, Швейцария в возрасте 62 лет и был похоронен в местности брисгау, баден-вuрттембирг, Германия.

sm.von 1152 bis 1186: mai le 1152 e o'o I le 1186

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Berthold IV. Herzog von Zähringen und Burgund nació aproximadamente 1125, en Urach, Oberamt Urach, Württemberg, Alemania, su papá, Conrad I von Zähringen, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Cleménce de Luxembourg- Namur, tenía 16 años. Se casó con Heilwig von Frohburg en 1155, en Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo y 2 hijas. En 1152, su ocupación registrada era regentschaft als herzog. Murió el 8 de diciembre de 1186, en Fribourg, Freiburg, Suiza, a la edad de 62 años, y fue sepultado en Breisgau, Baden-Württemberg, Alemania.
